clinical_presentation: Cognitive impairment (e.g., memory loss, executive dysfunction)
Focal neurological deficits (e.g., weakness, sensory changes)

differential_diagnoses.justification: The MRI findings of chronic small vessel ischemic changes and severe cerebral atrophy are highly characteristic of vascular dementia in an elderly patient.
Severe cerebral atrophy is a common feature of Alzheimer's disease, which frequently coexists with vascular pathology in older adults.
The observed cerebral atrophy and chronic ischemic changes are common findings in older individuals experiencing cognitive decline.

management_plan.immediate_actions: Clinical assessment for acute neurological deficits given DWI restriction indicating acute/subacute ischemia.
further_investigations: Comprehensive blood work (e.g., CBC, electrolytes, renal/liver function, thyroid, B12, folate, HbA1c, lipid panel)
Blood pressure monitoring and cardiovascular risk assessment
management_plan.follow_up_timeline: Urgent clinical review within days to assess for acute neurological symptoms and initiate comprehensive workup.

parsed.patient_explanation: Your brain scan shows that some of the small blood vessels in your brain have experienced some blockages, causing tiny areas of damage, some of which appear to be quite recent. We also see that your brain has shrunk more than is typical for your age. These changes are common in older adults and can affect memory, thinking, and balance. Additionally, there's some swelling in your nose, consistent with common rhinitis.

triage_priority.justification: The presence of DWI restriction indicates acute or subacute ischemic changes requiring urgent clinical correlation to assess for acute neurological deficits and initiate appropriate management.

red_flags: Restriction on DWI within multi-focal lesions
